browser_backend.note: Safari was used to create and copy a replacement API key; public Bearer API is now the active route.

media.alt_text: Thread Continuity graphic showing Codex, Claude, and Cursor connected as local agent history sources.

quality_gate.hidden_mechanism: Agent continuity breaks because useful work history is trapped inside separate harnesses.
quality_gate.public_safe_proof: Public repo plus local tests against Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and memory.
quality_gate.reader_takeaway: Search local evidence before asking a new agent to continue work.

posts.content:
AI agents are teammates with amnesia.

I built Thread Continuity so Codex, Claude Code, and Cursor can hand off local context with cited resume packets.

Local-only. Read-only. Open source.

Feedback welcome:
https://github.com/howdycarter/thread-continuity

AI agents are amazing, but they still have a basic problem:

they forget what happened in the other app.

Codex has one history. Claude Code has another. Cursor has another.

So I built Thread Continuity: a local, read-only way to find prior agent work and create a short resume packet for the next agent.

ELI10: it is a notebook for AI teammates with amnesia.

Would love feedback from builders who keep losing context between tools:
https://github.com/howdycarter/thread-continuity

AI coding agents have a continuity problem.

Not because they are weak.
Because the work is scattered.

One thread lives in Codex.
Another lives in Claude Code.
Another lives in Cursor.
Then a new agent shows up and has to guess what already happened.

I built Thread Continuity as a local, read-only bridge across that history.

ELI10: it is a notebook for AI teammates with amnesia.

It can search local Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and memory sources, then produce a compact resume packet with:

- what thread mattered
- where the work happened
- what the last known state was
- what files/proofs were referenced
- what still needs verification

The key design choice: old threads are evidence, not truth. The repo labels stale results and pushes the next agent to verify current code before acting.

Open source repo:
https://github.com/howdycarter/thread-continuity

I would love feedback from people building with multiple agent tools. What would make this genuinely useful in your workflow?

I built a small open-source tool for a problem I keep running into:

AI tools forget what happened in the other AI tool.

Codex knows one thread. Claude Code knows another. Cursor knows another. Then you ask a new agent to continue, and it starts from vibes instead of evidence.

Thread Continuity is my attempt to fix that locally.

ELI10: it is a notebook for AI teammates with amnesia.

It searches your local agent history, finds the relevant old work, and creates a short resume packet so the next agent can pick up the thread without guessing.

It is local-only, read-only, and open source.

Would love feedback:
https://github.com/howdycarter/thread-continuity

AI agents are smart teammates with amnesia.

One remembers Codex.
One remembers Claude Code.
One remembers Cursor.

Thread Continuity gives them a local notebook.

It finds prior agent work, cites the evidence, and creates a short resume packet so the next agent can continue without guessing.

Local-only. Read-only. Open source.

Feedback welcome:
https://github.com/howdycarter/thread-continuity

#AI #DeveloperTools #OpenSource #ProductDesign #DesignEngineering
